2603.14631.title: Anterior's Approach to Fairness Evaluation of Automated Prior Authorization System
2603.14631.paper_summary: Increasing staffing constraints and turnaround-time pressures in Prior authorization (PA) have led to increasing automation of decision systems to support PA review. Evaluating fairness in such systems poses unique challenges because legitimate clinical guidelines and medical necessity criteria often differ across demographic groups, making parity in approval rates an inappropriate fairness metric. We propose a fairness evaluation framework for prior authorization models based on model error rates rather than approval outcomes. Using 7,166 human-reviewed cases spanning 27 medical necessity guidelines, we assessed consistency in sex, age, race/ethnicity, and socioeconomic status. Our evaluation combined error-rate comparisons, tolerance-band analysis with a predefined $\pm$5 percentage-point margin, statistical power evaluation, and protocol-controlled logistic regression. Across most demographics, model error rates were consistent, and confidence intervals fell within the predefined tolerance band, indicating no meaningful performance differences. For race/ethnicity, point estimates remain small, but subgroup sample sizes were limited, resulting in wide confidence intervals and underpowered tests, with inconclusive evidence within the dataset we explored. These findings illustrate a rigorous and regulator-aligned approach to fairness evaluation in administrative healthcare AI systems.
2603.14631.paper_summary_zh: 增加的工作人員限制和周轉時間壓力使得事前授權（PA）的決策系統越來越自動化，以支持PA審查。評估這些系統的公平性帶來了獨特的挑戰，因為合法的臨床指導方針和醫療必要性標準在不同的人口群體之間往往存在差異，使得批准率的平等成為不適當的公平性指標。我們提出了一個基於模型錯誤率而非批准結果的事前授權模型公平性評估框架。使用7,166個經人類審查的案例，涵蓋27個醫療必要性指導方針，我們評估了性別、年齡、種族/民族和社會經濟地位的一致性。我們的評估結合了錯誤率比較、帶有預定$\pm$5個百分點範圍的容忍帶分析、統計檢驗力評估和協議控制的邏輯回歸。在大多數人口統計中，模型錯誤率是一致的，且置信區間落在預定的容忍帶內，表明沒有實質性的性能差異。對於種族/民族，點估計仍然較小，但子群體樣本大小有限，導致置信區間寬廣且檢驗力不足，在我們探索的數據集中證據不確定。這些發現展示了一種嚴謹且與監管機構對齊的行政醫療AI系統公平性評估方法。
2603.14631.author: Sai P. Selvaraj et.al.
2603.14631.authors: Sai P. Selvaraj, Khadija Mahmoud, Anuj Iravane
